title: eugenioides celastrineum
intro: Eugenioides celastrineum is a plant species in the family Celastraceae, which is native to tropical Asia. It is also known as the "wild forest hawthorn" and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant's extracts and leaves have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and preventing oxidative stress. Eugenioides celastrineum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
